message:
🌱 Add crossplane: Introduce a generic `Usage` type to enforce ordered deletion mission (#1602)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "crossplane-3393-introduce-a-generic-usage-type-to-enforce-ordered-deletion",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "crossplane: Introduce a generic `Usage` type to enforce ordered deletion",
+    "description": "Introduce a generic `Usage` type to enforce ordered deletion. Requested by 34+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current crossplane deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review crossplane configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

Crossplane is built on Kubernetes, which leans into eventual consistency."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Introduce a generic `Usage` type to enforce ordered deletion",
+        "description": "I have some concerns about solving the two use cases with a single API type which I detailed [here](https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/issues/4100#issuecomment-1568187121). I believe we can continue the discussion from there and close that ticket if we agree that solving them separately makes
```yaml
# A Reference used only for deletion ordering.
kind: Reference
metadata:
  name: release-uses-cluster
spec:
  # A 'blocking' reference prevents all of the 'to' (Cluster) resources from being
  # deleted until the 'from' resource (the Release) is gone.
  deletion: Blocking
  from:
    apiVersion: helm.crossplane.io/v1beta1
    kind: Release
    name: my-prometheus-chart
  to:
  - apiVersion: eks.upbound.io/v1beta1
    kind: Cluster
    name: my-cluster
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Introduce a generic `Usage` type to enforce ordered deletion" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I have some concerns about solving the two use cases with a single API type which I detailed [here](https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/issues/4100#issuecomment-1568187121). I believe we can continue the discussion from there and close that ticket if we agree that solving them separately makes more sense.

I want to focus on solving the deletion ordering with a dedicated `Usage` type here.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# A Reference used only for deletion ordering.
kind: Reference
metadata:
  name: release-uses-cluster
spec:
  # A 'blocking' reference prevents all of the 'to' (Cluster) resources from being
  # deleted until the 'from' resource (the Release) is gone.
  deletion: Blocking
  from:
    apiVersion: helm.crossplane.io/v1beta1
    kind: Release
    name: my-prometheus-chart
  to:
  - apiVersion: eks.upbound.io/v1beta1
    kind: Cluster
    name: my-cluster",
+        "# A reference used only to derive field values.
apiVersion: crossplane.io/v1alpha1
type: Reference
metadata:
  name: subnet-to-vpc
spec:
  # A 'non-blocking' reference is the default. The VPC can be deleted before
  # the Subnet.
  deletion: NonBlocking
  from:
    apiVersion: network.aws.crossplane.io/v1alpha2
    kind: Subnet
    name: my-subnet
    # Note the optional fieldPath is specified here and below.
    fieldPath: spec.forProvider.vpcId
  to:
  # An array to support the case where a reference is derived from multiple
  # fields in multiple objects? This would likely end up pretty close to
  # Composition patches, with transforms etc. We'd probably want to support
  # selectors here too, not only explicit references.
  - apiVersion: network.aws.crossplane.io/v1alpha2
    kind: VPC",
+        "apiVersion: apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Usage
metadata:
  name: release-uses-cluster
spec: 
  of: 
    # This could still be an array, however, considering existing use cases,
    # I believe we would rarely need arrays here and multiple `Usage` objects 
    # could be created instead of having this as an array.
    apiVersion: eks.upbound.io/v1beta1
    kind: Cluster
    name: my-cluster
    # implement selectors
  by:
    apiVersion: helm.crossplane.io/v1beta1
    kind: Release
    name: my-prometheus-chart
    # implement selectors"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "crossplane",
+      "graduated",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "crossplane"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "expert",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/issues/3393",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane"
+    },
+    "reactions": 34,
+    "comments": 31,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
